title: Dense clusters of primes in subsets

sec:1:19.summary: Prime $k$-tuples conjecture for general admissible families of linear functions $L_i(n) = a_i n + b_i$.

thm:3.1.summary: Generalized framework: under hypotheses on $(\mathcal{A}, \mathcal{P}, \mathcal{L}, B, x, \theta)$ — $\mathcal{A}$ a set of integers with reasonable density, $\mathcal{P}$ a set of primes equidistributed in $\mathcal{A}$ — for $k \ge C(\alpha,\theta)$ admissible linear forms $L_i$, infinitely many $n \in \mathcal{A}$ have $\gg \log k$ of $L_i(n) \in \mathcal{P}$. The reusable engine.

thm:3.2.summary: Small gaps in short intervals: for $\gg x \exp(-\sqrt{\log x})$ integers $x_0 \in [x,2x]$, $\pi(x_0+y) - \pi(x_0) \gg \log y$. Quantitative density of intervals containing many primes.

thm:3.3.summary: Clusters of primes in arithmetic progressions: for $q \le (\log x)^{1-\varepsilon}$, there are $\gg \pi(x)/(2q)^{\exp(Cm)}$ consecutive primes $p_n,\ldots,p_{n+m}$ all $\equiv a \pmod{q}$ with $p_{n+m}-p_n \le \varepsilon \log x$. Direct application of thm:3.1.

thm:3.4.summary: Primes in short intervals: for $x^{7/12+\varepsilon} \le y \le x$ and admissible $\mathcal{L}$ of size $k = \exp(O(m))$, $\#\{n \in [x,x+y]: \ge m \text{ of } L_i(n) \text{ prime}\} \gg y/(\log x)^k$. Uses Huxley's prime-number theorem in short intervals as the equidistribution input.

thm:3.5.summary: Chebotarev / Galois version: for $K/\mathbb{Q}$ Galois with conjugacy class $\mathcal{C} \subset \mathrm{Gal}(K/\mathbb{Q})$, there are infinitely many $m$-clusters of primes $p$ with prescribed Artin symbol. Maynard's sieve applied to primes split in extensions.

prop:6.1.summary: Sieve weight asymptotic. For weights $w_n$ built from a smooth $F$ on the simplex, the $S_1, S_2$ sums evaluate in terms of $I_k(F), J_k(F)$ generalized to the ambient set $\mathcal{A}$ with multiplicative-density correction $(B/\varphi(B))^k\,\mathfrak{S}_B(\mathcal{L})$. The $k$-D Selberg sieve, generalized.

lem:8.1.summary: Singular series bounds: $\mathfrak{S}_B(\mathcal{L}) \ge \exp(-Ck)$ and a flexible perturbation lemma controlling $\Delta_L/\varphi(\Delta_L)$ sums. Replaces the $W$-trick from 1311.4600.

lem:8.2.summary: Sieve-weight perturbation: $y_{\mathbf{s}} = y_{\mathbf{r}} + O(T_k Y_{\mathbf{r}} \log A / \log R)$ when $\mathbf{r}$ and $\mathbf{s}$ differ by a single coordinate scaling. Replaces lem:5.3 of 1311.4600 in the more general setting.

prop:9.1.summary: $S_1$ evaluation: $\sum_{n \in \mathcal{A}(x)} w_n = (1+o(1)) (B/\varphi(B))^k\,\mathfrak{S}_B(\mathcal{L})\,\#\mathcal{A}(x)\,(\log R)^k\,I_k(F)$. The non-prime side, generalized to $\mathcal{A}$.

prop:9.2.summary: $S_2$ evaluation: under a level-of-distribution hypothesis on $(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{L}, \mathcal{A})$ at level $x^\theta$, $\sum \mathbf{1}_{\mathcal{P}}(L(n))\,w_n$ has the expected $J_k(F)$ asymptotic. The prime side, generalized to $\mathcal{A}$ and $\mathcal{P}$.

prop:9.4.summary: Almost-primality control for the auxiliary smooth-number set $\mathcal{S}(\xi;D)$. Replaces direct $W$-trick filtering with a sieve-friendly version.

prop:9.5.summary: Small-prime contribution bound: weighted count of small prime factors $p < x^\rho$ of $L(n)$ is $O(\rho^2 k^4 (\log k)^2\,\mathfrak{S}\,\#\mathcal{A}(x)\,(\log R)^k\,I_k(F))$. Removes contamination.

edges.note: Sieve weight asymptotic provides the existence of the $M_k$-style ratio under generalized hypotheses.
thm:3.1 is the unconditional partial result for the generalized prime $k$-tuples conjecture in subsets $\mathcal{A}$.
Specialization to $\mathcal{A} = $ arithmetic progression, $\mathcal{P} = $ primes in that progression.
Specialization to short intervals, using Huxley's PNT for the equidistribution hypothesis.
Specialization to $\mathcal{A} = \mathbb{Z}$, $\mathcal{P} = $ primes with prescribed Artin symbol. Chebotarev supplies equidistribution.
Same singular-series asymptotics on the prime side.
Same perturbation in the small-prime estimate.

main_results.description: thm:3.1 is the generalized framework. thm:3.2-3.5 are four corollary applications: short intervals, arithmetic progressions, short intervals with primes, and Chebotarev/Galois. The Chebotarev variant (thm:3.5) is genuinely new — opens prime-distribution-in-extensions to the Maynard-Tao machinery.

generalized_sieve_framework.description: prop:6.1 is the abstract version of 1311.4600:prop:4.1 — sieve weight asymptotic with abstract $\mathcal{A}, \mathcal{P}, B$ replacing the integers, primes, and $W$. This is the architectural refactor that makes thm:3.1 possible.

asymptotic_engine.description: Section 9 propositions implement the $S_1, S_2$ evaluations under the abstract hypotheses; lem:8.1, lem:8.2 supply the singular-series and perturbation tools (replacing the $W$-trick).

Maynard:smallgaps.title: Small gaps between primes
Maynard:smallgaps.role: method_source — this paper is the abstract framework version of 1311.4600

Tao:blogClusters.title: Polymath proposal: bounded gaps between primes (Tao blog)
Tao:blogClusters.role: context — Tao's parallel work on the same multidim sieve

Huxley:1972.title: On the difference between consecutive primes
Huxley:1972.role: foundational — PNT in short intervals, used in thm:3.4

Chebotarev:1926.title: Chebotarev's density theorem
Chebotarev:1926.role: foundational — equidistribution of Frobenius classes; used in thm:3.5
